title: Hall effect in multi-leg bosonic ladders
authors: Edmond Orignac, Roberta Citro, Thierry Giamarchi
abstract: We use bosonization to analyze the ground state Hall response of interacting bosonic N-leg ladders threaded by a flux. We derive an explicit expression of the Hall imbalance in a perturbative expansion in the band curvature, retaining fully the interactions. For small magnetic field the Hall resistance is proportional to the derivative of the logarithm of the charge stiffness with respect to density, generalizing the result obtained in the two leg case. We also consider the effect of temperature, and establish that at low temperature, corrections to the Hall resistance are exponentially small in the Meissner phase.
summary: This paper provides a theoretical generalization of the Hall effect in multi-leg bosonic ladders using bosonization. It establishes a universal connection between Hall resistance and the density derivative of charge stiffness and characterizes how temperature affects the Hall response in Meissner and Vortex phases.
main_problem: The study aims to generalize the Hall effect and Hall resistance from two-leg to N-leg bosonic ladders and to understand the temperature dependence of the Hall response in different magnetic flux regimes.
main_result: The Hall resistance is found to be proportional to the derivative of the logarithm of the charge stiffness with respect to density, a result that is robust for N-leg ladders. Additionally, Hall imbalance corrections are exponentially small in the Meissner phase at low temperatures but follow a power law in the Vortex phase.
method: The authors use bosonization and perturbation theory in the band curvature, employing a basis transformation with Hahn/Chebyshev polynomials to decouple modes and Green's function techniques for temperature-dependent analysis.
model_or_system: The system consists of interacting bosonic N-leg ladders threaded by a magnetic flux (artificial gauge fields), modeled using a bosonized Hamiltonian including Tomonaga-Luttinger Liquid terms, transverse interchain hopping, and band curvature.
key_observables: Hall resistance (Rh), Hall voltage (Vh), Hall imbalance (Ph), and charge stiffness (uK).
important_parameters: Magnetic flux (B), temperature (T), particle density (rho0), transverse hopping (t_perp), and Tomonaga-Luttinger parameter (K).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes a perturbative expansion in band curvature and focuses on the low-field limit and the validity of the bosonization approach for the ladder geometries.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the temperature dependence of the Hall imbalance in the Meissner phase, Vortex phase, and at the commensurate-incommensurate transition for various magnetic flux values.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of N-leg ladders, applies bosonization and basis transformations to derive Hall resistance formulas, analyzes the temperature dependence of the Hall imbalance in different flux regimes, and discusses the robustness of the results.
why_interesting: This paper is highly relevant for many-body dynamics and AMO physics as it provides analytical predictions for transport properties (Hall effect) in synthetic gauge fields, which are a key component of ultracold atom experiments in optical lattices.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Temperature dependence of the Hall imbalance in the Meissner phase (Red) in the Vortex phase (Blue) and at the commensurate-incommensurate transition (Green).
